name: Stefado (Greek-style Oven Beef Stew)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the chuck steak on a rack in a broiling pan and broil, turning once, until rare, about 5 minutes on each side.
2. Cut into 1-inch cubes using a slotted spoon.
3. Heat olive oil in a 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat. Add the cubed chuck and sauté for 5 minutes.
4. Transfer meat to a 1 1/2-quart casserole, reserving pan drippings.
5. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. In the skillet, stir water, tomato paste, vinegar, cinnamon stick, salt, cumin seed, and pepper into the reserved pan drippings. Bring to a boil, stirring to scrape up any browned particles.
7. Pour the mixture over the meat in the casserole. Cover and bake for 1 1/2 hours.
8. Add the parboiled onions, cover, and continue baking until the meat is tender, about 30 minutes longer.
9. Remove and discard the cinnamon stick.
10. Top with crumbled feta cheese and bake, uncovered, until the cheese is softened and heated through, about 5 minutes.
Makes 4 servings.
